gold_change_reason: The seed solution did not handle multiple tumors, placeholders beyond isdigit/isalpha, whitespace/blank lines, missing columns, or consistency checks. The scaled task adds all these requirements. The new code implements robust parsing with try-except for numeric conversion, skips blank lines, pads missing columns, and raises ValueError on dimension mismatch in C.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include input with multiple tumor fractions and verify nested list output for C and p*.
Scaled executable cases must include variants with placeholder values and verify None output.
Scaled executable cases must include inputs that trigger consistency checks (mismatched dimensions) and expect exceptions.
Scaled executable cases must include files with blank lines and extra whitespace, expecting correct parsing.
Scaled executable cases must include various placeholder strings beyond simple alphabetic/non-digit.
Scaled executable cases must include inputs with missing fields and verify appropriate default values.
implementation_notes: Handles multiple tumors by transposing colon-separated groups of comma-separated values for C, and for p* wraps all probabilities into a single inner list (as per seed logic).
Placeholders in C and p* are detected by trying int() and float() conversion; if ValueError, returns None, covering 'NA', '?', '.', 'na', etc.
Consistency check: for each C group, the number of comma-separated values must equal the number of tumor fractions (n_tumors) when n_tumors > 0; otherwise raises ValueError.
Whitespace robustness: strips each line and skips blank lines; individual fields are stripped during conversion.
Missing columns: pads body list to 4 columns with empty strings; empty mu string yields mu_normal=None and mu_tumors=[]; empty C or p* yields empty list.
assumptions: The input file always has at least one non-empty data line after header.
For p* with multiple tumors, the expected output is a list containing one inner list with all probabilities (matching seed behavior and oracle).
When mu is empty (no tumors), C and p* are parsed as if there is one tumor (single flat lists).
